commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an Ace Data Cloud external provider catalog entry, a provider docs page, and docs navigation/redirects.

PR surface: Docs +132, Other +57. Total +189 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: the pinned package metadata and runtime show optionKey acedatacloudApiKey while OpenClaw reads opts[params.optionKey], and the PR body includes the failing non-interactive onboarding command. I did not run live Ace Data Cloud API calls.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A reviewer needs a corrected external package release and a maintainer trust/provenance decision before this repo can safely pin the provider.

target.title: docs(providers): register Ace Data Cloud third-party provider plugin
target.body:
## Summary

Adds [`@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider) as an external provider entry so the package becomes discoverable through `openclaw plugins install` and the onboarding wizard, and ships a `/providers/acedatacloud` docs page describing setup.

[Ace Data Cloud](https://platform.acedata.cloud) is a unified, OpenAI-compatible AI gateway. The plugin exposes:

- **Chat** — 60+ curated models plus passthrough for any upstream Ace Data Cloud model id, all routed through `https://api.acedata.cloud/v1`.
- **Web Search** — Google SERP across `search`, `images`, `news`, `videos`, `maps`, `places`, registered against the `webSearchProviders` contract and reusing the same API key.

## What this PR changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `scripts/lib/official-external-provider-catalog.json` | Add `@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider` entry (`source: "external"`, `kind: "provider"`), with a chat auth-choice for the `text-inference` onboarding scope and a `webSearchProviders` registration. |
| `docs/providers/acedatacloud.md` | New provider docs page, modeled on `/providers/openrouter` and `/providers/pixverse`. |
| `docs/docs.json` | Add `providers/acedatacloud` to the Providers nav group and add an `/acedatacloud -> /providers/acedatacloud` redirect. |

## Fixes made after ClawSweeper review

This PR now includes all catalog/docs fixes ClawSweeper called out, plus one additional onboarding bug found during re-validation:

1. Pin `install.npmSpec` to `@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider@2026.5.33` and add `expectedIntegrity`.
2. Change the web-search `credentialPath` to `plugins.entries.acedatacloud.config.webSearch.apiKey`.
3. Update the provider docs snippet to use the same plugin-scoped `webSearch` config path.
4. Fix the related docs card link from `/tools/web-search` to `/tools/web`.
5. Align the catalog auth `optionKey` with Commander parsing for `--acedata-api-key` by using `acedataApiKey` instead of `acedatacloudApiKey`.

## Real behavior proof: current validated state

**Environment used for re-validation**

- macOS 26.2 (arm64)
- global host CLI: `openclaw 2026.5.30-beta.1 (effbaeb)`
- isolated temp home via `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-ace-proof.*`
- provider catalog file swapped locally to this PR's current JSON so the installed host exercised the exact catalog metadata under review

**What was validated successfully**

1. `openclaw plugins install npm:@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider` succeeds in a fresh isolated home.
2. `openclaw plugins inspect acedatacloud` reports:
   - `Status: loaded`
   - `Capabilities: text-inference + web-search`
   - `Version: 2026.5.33`
3. `openclaw onboard --help` advertises both:
   - `--auth-choice acedatacloud-api-key`
   - `--acedata-api-key <key>`
4. `openclaw infer web providers` lists `acedatacloud` as an available web-search provider with env vars `ACEDATA_API_KEY` / `ACEDATACLOUD_API_KEY`.

Representative output from the isolated run:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-ace-proof... openclaw plugins install npm:@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider
Installed plugin: acedatacloud

$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-ace-proof... openclaw plugins inspect acedatacloud
Status: loaded
Capabilities:
text-inference: acedatacloud
web-search: acedatacloud
Version: 2026.5.33

$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-ace-proof... openclaw onboard --help
--acedata-api-key <key>  Ace Data Cloud API key (https://platform.acedata.cloud)
--auth-choice <choice>   ... acedatacloud-api-key ...
```

## Current blocker discovered during stronger proof collection

While collecting the requested end-to-end onboarding/web-search proof, I found that the **published provider package itself** still ships the same `optionKey` mismatch that I fixed in this PR's catalog metadata.

The published `@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider@2026.5.33` currently uses:

- `optionKey: "acedatacloudApiKey"`
- `cliFlag: "--acedata-api-key"`

But Commander parses `--acedata-api-key` into `acedataApiKey`, so a fresh isolated host that installs the published package still fails non-interactive onboarding with:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-ace-proof... openclaw onboard --non-interactive --accept-risk \
  --auth-choice acedatacloud-api-key --acedata-api-key <redacted>
Missing --acedata-api-key (or ACEDATA_API_KEY in env, or existing acedatacloud API-key profile).
```

That means the remaining blocker is **not** this repo's catalog/docs wiring anymore; it is the already-published provider artifact.

## Follow-up required before this PR can honestly claim full onboarding proof

A new `@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider` release is needed with the provider package's own `optionKey` fixed to `acedataApiKey`, followed by a bump of this PR's pinned npm version + integrity to that released artifact.

I fixed that provider-side mismatch locally in `AceDataCloud/OpenClawProvider` and verified it with:

```text
pnpm build
pnpm test
# 14 tests passed
```

But until that corrected artifact is published, I cannot truthfully provide the exact end-to-end onboarding + web-search proof against the reviewed npm package that ClawSweeper asked for.

## Notes on earlier proof

The earlier PR body contained direct package-install chat proof. That still demonstrates raw chat inference for the provider package itself, but it bypasses the exact onboarding/web-search surfaces under review here, so it is not sufficient as the sole merge proof for this PR.
target.author: acedatacloud-dev
